compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
# Event-sourced enrichment queue: cert-triggered enqueue, 60-min soak, latest-wins, lock-guarded claim

## Why
Enrichment must be async and off the hot write path. rebar's event store is already a durable, shared, serialized-write queue substrate — better than a bolted-on SQLite/broker (which cannot live on a shared path nor be visible to all clients). Modeling the queue as sidecar events makes "a new certification replaces the old entry" the natural latest-wins reduce.

## What
Three sidecar events keyed by `ticket_id`, reduced to queue state:
- `ENQUEUE_ENRICH` `{ticket_id, not_before_ns}` appended on plan-review CERTIFICATION, `not_before_ns = cert + SOAK` (SOAK default 60 min). A re-cert appends a new one that supersedes the prior (latest-wins by HLC) and bumps `not_before_ns` — the soak/debounce as a stored timestamp filtered at drain, NOT an in-process timer.
- `CLAIM_ENRICH` `{ticket_id, drainer_id, lease_expires_ns}`, `DONE_ENRICH` `{ticket_id}`.
- Reducer `pending_enrichment(now_ns)` → entries past soak, unclaimed-or-lease-expired, with no later DONE.

## Atomicity + claim (accurate to the store's ACTUAL locking model — DERISKED)
`append_event` (`_commands/_seam.py`) SELF-SERIALIZES every write: it goes through
`stage_and_commit`, which acquires the store write lock (`_store/lock.py::write_lock`) for its
own append. So a claim CANNOT hold `lock.acquire` across its own `append_event` — the
non-reentrant mkdir leg of the lock would deadlock/timeout. The claim primitive is therefore
OPTIMISTIC (the epic's stated model: "one drainer lands, losers retry"), not a held-lock
read-check-append.
- **Enqueue placement (no false atomicity):** `sign_plan_review` writes the SIGNATURE via `sign_manifest`→`append_event`, which takes its OWN per-event lock; there is no shared held-lock spanning cert+enqueue, and refactoring the signing path to provide one is out of scope. So `ENQUEUE_ENRICH` is a SEPARATE append immediately after `sign_plan_review` returns. Correctness does NOT depend on atomicity: the S4 drain's self-healing fallback treats any certified ticket with an absent/stale digest as enqueable, so a crash between cert and ENQUEUE cannot permanently miss it.
- **Race-free claim (OPTIMISTIC append + arbitration):** the drainer re-reduces the ticket's queue state and, only if still pending (unclaimed or lease-expired), appends `CLAIM_ENRICH` (with a fresh `lease_expires_ns`) via `append_event` (which self-serializes under the store write lock). It then ARBITRATES: among the `CLAIM_ENRICH` events after the latest `ENQUEUE_ENRICH` whose lease is STILL LIVE, the earliest `(timestamp, uuid)` wins. THIS drainer won iff it is that earliest live claim; losers re-read and pick another. Filtering contenders by live lease is what makes lease expiry self-healing (an expired prior claim is not a contender). Exactly one drainer lands per soak window.
Lease `LEASE_TTL` (default 15 min); a claim with `lease_expires_ns < now` is treated as unclaimed on the next drain (self-healing, no separate reaper process). Defense-in-depth fallback (owned/implemented by the S4 drain, see below): the drain ALSO treats a ticket that is certified but whose digest is absent/stale as implicitly enqueable, so even a crash between cert and ENQUEUE cannot permanently miss it.

## Scope (exact edit targets)
- `src/rebar/_store/event_append.py` `EVENT_TYPES` (+ StoreError message): add the three types. `src/rebar/reducer/_version.py`: add to `_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES` (sidecar; like REVIEW_RESULT/TICKET_DIGEST).
- Enqueue hook in the `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/` certification path (`attest.sign_plan_review`, best-effort after the SIGNATURE append), using the HLC clock (`hlc.physical_now()`) for `not_before_ns`. No held-lock cert-critical-section (the enqueue is a separate append; correctness rests on the self-healing fallback, not atomicity).
- Config field target `src/rebar/llm/config.py`: `overlap_soak_min` (60), `overlap_lease_ttl_min` (15) on `LLMConfig` (`[tool.rebar.llm]`, `REBAR_LLM_OVERLAP_*`) — LLM-feature tunables, not `VerifyConfig`.
- This story owns ENQUEUE + CLAIM + the reducer. It does NOT own DONE-emission, the growth-bound retention, or the self-healing scan — those require the drain runner and are OWNED BY S4 (leggy-heedless-mosquito).

## Dependencies
S2 (wispy-cheerless-tick) for the digest freshness the enqueue/drain consult. S4 owns the drain that appends DONE + runs the fallback scan.

## Testing
- `tests/unit/test_enrich_queue.py`: `test_enqueue_and_recert` (not_before = cert+SOAK; re-cert supersedes+bumps); `test_soak_and_latest_wins` (pending honors soak + ≤1/ticket); `test_claim_race` (two claimers in one critical section → exactly one CLAIM lands); `test_lease_reclaim` (expired lease reclaimable, idempotent).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Certifying appends `ENQUEUE_ENRICH` with `not_before_ns = cert + SOAK` (a separate append after `sign_plan_review`); re-cert supersedes+bumps; `pytest ...::test_enqueue_and_recert`. A crash between cert and enqueue is recovered by the S4 self-healing scan (no permanent miss).
- [ ] `pending_enrichment(now)` returns only past-soak, unclaimed-or-expired, ≤1/ticket; `::test_soak_and_latest_wins`.
- [ ] Two concurrent claimers → exactly one wins via optimistic append + earliest-live-lease arbitration; the loser re-reads and picks another; `::test_claim_one_winner`.
- [ ] Lease-expired claim reclaimable on next drain (self-healing); re-processing idempotent; `::test_lease_reclaim`.
- [ ] The three event types are in `EVENT_TYPES` + `_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES`. (DONE-emission, growth-bound, and the self-healing scan are S4's ACs, not this story's.)
compiled_state.title: Event-sourced enrichment queue: cert-triggered enqueue, 60-min soak, latest-wins, optimistic claim
